title: Purple Lipstick
wikitext:
[]: Purple Lipstick.             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Mar 08, 2011 10:52 pm I apologize ahead of time for not being able to supply a screenshot, and I must ask that somebody with the to-be-described shot please support the discussion. I must also let you know that even I'm not above fanwanking. In episode 2, while Ritsuko is talking to Misato about Shinji and the Hedgehog's Dilemma, we get a shot of Ritsuko's ashtray, each butt covered in her lipstick. At least two of the (many) cigarettes, however, feature purple lipstick, in contrast with Ritsuko's typical red. Only one other character in the series wears purple lipstick, and, by no coincidence, it's Naoko Akagi. It's clearly established that Naoko had a sexual affair with Gendo Ikari, and it is later made clear that Ritsuko does as well. By this establishment, we can pretty much associate Naoko as "that chick who slept with Gendo." So, when we take the cosmetics of that chick who slept with Gendo—cosmetics being a tool to appear more attractive to, in this case, the opposite sex—and slap them onto another character, who we later find to have a similar relationship with the same person, can't we then consider this to be a hint as to the nature of Ritsuko and Gendo's relationship? Discuss!

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Tue Mar 08, 2011 11:28 pm Here is the screenshot you were after, however note that the conversation about the Hedgehog's Dilemma takes place in Episode 03. Good composition here - the papers, the Yin&Yang-ish cat figures and the ash tray are in the sweet spots of a nice reverse L-shaped composition; leading our eyes from the former to the latter. Although the purple-lined ciggy butts are at the edge of the frame, it does suggest in advance (by association with Naoko's purple lipstick) that Ritsuko and Gendo are having an affair, but that they may have done it in that very office! (or at least, Ritsuko was in that room before or after each 'encounter' with Gendo). As for why purple lipstick... as I said, it's used as a device to suggest in advance; but for Ritsuko's reasons for using it... I might be going into Ep13/Ep21' territory here, but doesn't she look up to her mother a great deal? Maybe this is meant to echo that Ritsuko wants to be like her on more than a professional level, but on the level of a woman, too. Ironically, the part of Naoko/Magi betrays Ritsuko in 26' is her woman-side OH WAIT WHAT HAVE WE STUMBLED UPON As for Gendo, I don't think he'd care what colour lipstick Rits used... but hey, if he notices it's similar to Naoko's, then it's a sign he's got her in the palm of his hand like he had her mother, right? [Edit: Seriously, the idea of Ritsuko using the purple lipstick because she wants to be like her mother on a womanly-level, only to be betrayed by that section of the Magi in '26 is just too awesome.]

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Mar 08, 2011 11:49 pm EvangelionFan wrote: [Edit: Seriously, the idea of Ritsuko using the purple lipstick because she wants to be like her mother on a womanly-level, only to be betrayed by that section of the Magi in '26 is just too awesome.]

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Mar 09, 2011 9:11 am Some of the butts don't have any lipstick imprints. Is the room private, or is it accessible to any Nerv employee? The purple imprints might stem from some other female worker who smokes there.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 09, 2011 9:41 am Maybe Ritsuko occasionally wears purple lipstick or no lipstick at all? Just because she isn't shown wearing it during the show doesn't mean she never does.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Mar 09, 2011 9:58 am It's a cool idea, but I think it's kind of reaching.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Mar 09, 2011 11:38 am EvangelionFan wrote: [Edit: Seriously, the idea of Ritsuko using the purple lipstick because she wants to be like her mother on a womanly-level, only to be betrayed by that section of the Magi in '26 is just too awesome.]

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Mar 09, 2011 12:13 pm Allemann wrote: Some of the butts don't have any lipstick imprints. Is the room private, or is it accessible to any Nerv employee? The purple imprints might stem from some other female worker who smokes there.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Mar 09, 2011 2:59 pm I was under the impression that, despite cavorting with the same man, Ritsuko was pretty desperate to separate herself from her mother, what with bleaching her hair and hating to be thought of as "Dr. Akaghi's daughter" and stuff. The purple to red ratio (two to a buttload) in her ashtray may represent the small vestiges of buried Naokoness in her psyche despite her visible efforts to distance herself from.... okay, seriously this is just too much wanking, even for EvaGeeks.

[]: Re: Purple Lipstick.             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Mar 09, 2011 3:56 pm Sachi wrote: can't we then consider this to be a hint as to the nature of Ritsuko and Gendo's relationship?

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Wed Mar 09, 2011 4:01 pm EvangelionFan wrote: As for Gendo, I don't think he'd care what colour lipstick Rits used...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 09, 2011 4:01 pm Anno plays a lot with color purple in series (not that Naoko but also with the way Yui's shirt changes mid-scene in EoE) and I remember always wondering - since I first saw it - "why are there cigs with purple lipsticks there?" it always caught my attention. But I never could figure any reason for this. I don't think idea that they were some random nameless never-seen-on-screen chick's cigs is plausible as ashtray is one of those objects Anno carefully links with certain character, in this case Ritsuko. As of now I would say Sachi has really stumbled on something here, despite my usual skepticism about fanwank. This looks like genuine, meaningful hint to me.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Mar 09, 2011 4:45 pm That would be a ridiculously buried hint though, especially concerning Naoko won't even appear until 19 episodes later.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 09, 2011 4:55 pm NemZ wrote: That would be a ridiculously buried hint though, especially concerning Naoko won't even appear until 19 episodes later.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Mar 09, 2011 5:08 pm Xard wrote: then again, do we see Ritsuko ever using purple lipstick?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Mar 09, 2011 5:20 pm Xard wrote: I think scripts would be pretty useful here,

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 09, 2011 5:28 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: You do realize that not all directors/screenwriters spell out their intentions in scripts, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Mar 09, 2011 5:31 pm If I recall, Ritsuko didn't wear lipstick at least one time when she visited Misato and Shinji at their apartment. It can be guessed she uses cosmetics in more formal situations like the working place. The butts without the impresses can be explained if Ritsuko is a chain smoker - the impressive butt heap does allude to that - she wears out her lipstick with repeated pressing of her lips at the cigarette paper. The purple impresses can also be discolorations; or they ran out of red ink for that scene; or Anno wanted variety for the sake of it. If Ritsuko never had purple lipstick on her mouth (and I'm aligned towards the negative answer), the alleged symbolism is very hard to establish.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Wed Mar 09, 2011 6:32 pm So was Misato ever shown wearing purple lipstick (maybe along with her lavender perfume)? I think she even mentioned to Kaji once that the only time she ever smokes is right after... Just sayin' I mean, this is supposed to be fanwank, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Mar 09, 2011 7:40 pm Xard wrote: However due to nature of animation production the really important details and bits are often given specific mentions
